reasoning: Q1 iteration 0 achieves correct results but is 1.9x slower than DuckDB (164.4ms vs 86.4ms). The dominant bottleneck is scan_filter (89.4ms, 54% of total), with concrete root causes: scalar filter evaluation, dictionary decoding before filtering (wasteful for 98% selectivity), and float arithmetic in aggregation. The evaluation identifies 7 actionable optimizations with proven techniques; implementing the top 3 (SIMD filtering, filter-before-decode, integer aggregation) should reduce scan_filter from 89.4ms to ~35-50ms, bringing total time to ~100-115ms and approaching DuckDB's 108.8ms average.
focus_areas: SIMD vectorization for predicate evaluation (88.4ms → 50-60ms expected gain)
Filter-first pattern with late materialization (eliminate 2% wasted dictionary lookups + defer numeric column accesses)
Integer-domain aggregation (remove 359M FP operations from hot loop, eliminate Kahan summation overhead)
Utilize AVX-512 SIMD available on target hardware for 16-way parallelism in predicate evaluation
strategy_notes: Aggressively optimize the scan_filter phase which dominates runtime. (1) Use AVX-512 vectorized comparison (_mm512_cmple_epi32) to evaluate 16 shipdate values per instruction, producing bitmask selection vector in 256-row batches (fits L1 cache). (2) Apply filter BEFORE dictionary decoding: predicate evaluation first → selection vector → decode l_returnflag/l_linestatus only for matching rows. (3) Perform aggregation entirely in integer domain (quantities and prices stored as int64_t scaled by 100): compute disc_price = price * (100 - disc) / 100 and charge = disc_price * (100 + tax) / 100 using integer arithmetic; convert to double only in final output. This eliminates 4 FP divisions + 2 FP multiplies per row (359M FP ops total), replacing with integer multiply/divide which is ~2x faster and removes Kahan summation overhead. Combined, these 3 optimizations target the hot loop efficiency: vectorized filtering reduces iterations needed, filter-before-decode improves cache locality and eliminates wasted work, integer aggregation removes FP bottleneck. Expected cumulative reduction: 45-65ms (scan_filter: 89.4ms → 30-40ms), bringing total to ~100-115ms range. The techniques are conservative (proven in DuckDB, PostgreSQL, and academic literature) and hardware-aligned (AVX-512 available, HDD with MADV_SEQUENTIAL acceptable).
notes: Query has perfect correctness (4 rows matched). No critical fixes needed. Remaining iterations: 2. If these 3 optimizations yield expected gains, iteration 2 can address secondary bottlenecks (perfect hash for 4 groups, zone map block skipping) to further narrow the gap. Do NOT fear implementing all 3 simultaneously — they are orthogonal optimizations (SIMD doesn't conflict with filter ordering or integer aggregation), so Query Optimizer can apply all changes in one pass and test the result.
